name: ThemeSpec

unions.name: DarkModeStrategy
unions.comment:
Defines the dark mode strategy.

  - `SystemStrategy` uses the user system settings.
  - `ClassStrategy` uses the presence of a CSS class to determine dark mode.

aliases.name: Theme
aliases.type: { fontTitle : String.String, fontText : String.String, fontCode : String.String, borderRadius : Basics.Int, borderRadiusLarge : Basics.Int, focus : String.String, background : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor, color : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor, highlight : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor, success : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor, warning : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor, danger : ThemeSpec.ThemeColor }

aliases.name: ThemeColor
aliases.type: { base : String.String, light : String.String, dark : String.String, tint : String.String, contrast : String.String, shadow : String.String }

values.name: borderRadius
values.comment:
`var(--tmspc-border-radius)`

Usually used for the border radius of small elements. (e.g. buttons)

values.name: borderRadiusLarge
values.comment:
`var(--tmspc-border-radius-large)`

Usually used for the border radius of large elements. (e.g. cards)

values.name: globalProviderWithDarkMode
values.type: { light : ThemeSpec.Theme, dark : ThemeSpec.Theme, strategy : ThemeSpec.DarkModeStrategy } -> Html.Html msg

values.name: provider
values.type: ThemeSpec.Theme -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: providerWithDarkMode
values.type: { light : ThemeSpec.Theme, dark : ThemeSpec.Theme, strategy : ThemeSpec.DarkModeStrategy } -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: sample
values.comment: Renders an element that showcases all the current theme's colors and settings.

values.name: toString
values.comment: Use this if you want to create your own custom providers. This should only be useful for theme-spec interop library authors.
